reforms.description: The April 1983 Federal budget introduced a Special Recovery Program (SRP) aimed at providing immediate economic stimulus through direct public works expenditure and fiscal concessions to stimulate private fixed capital formation, including enhanced investment tax credits and the establishment of Special Recovery Funds for investment and export financing.
reforms.component_name: Investment Tax Credits and Recovery Funds
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The program is designed to stimulate private investment and economic growth through fiscal incentives.
reforms.status_evidence: The program was implemented as part of the 1983 Federal budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change aimed at boosting private investment.
reforms.source_quote: A Special Recovery Program (SRP) is costed at some $4.8 billion over the four fiscal years, split equally between direct public works expenditure and fiscal concessions aimed at stimulating private fixed capital formation.

reforms.description: The Federal budget promotes technology and R&D expenditure as part of its measures to stimulate economic growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Promoting technology and R&D is expected to enhance innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The budget includes measures to promote technology and R&D expenditure.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, it is not as transformative as the Special Recovery Program.
reforms.source_quote: Measures to stimulate residential investment, including promotion of technology and R&D expenditure.
